questions.questionText: Using only a ruler and compasses, what is the first step to construct the perpendicular bisector of a line segment AB?
options: Draw a circle with center A and radius AB.
Place the compass point on A and draw an arc above and below the line.
Measure the length of AB and divide by two.
Draw a line from A to the midpoint of AB.
questions.explanation: To construct a perpendicular bisector, place the compass point on one endpoint (A), open it to more than half the length of the segment, and draw arcs above and below the line.

questions.questionText: To construct an angle of 60 degrees at point P on a line, after drawing an arc from P that intersects the line at Q, what is the next step?
options: Place the compass point on Q, with the same radius, and draw an arc intersecting the first arc.
Place the compass point on P, with a different radius, and draw another arc.
Draw a line perpendicular to the original line at P.
Measure 60 degrees with a protractor.
questions.explanation: For a 60-degree angle, maintain the same compass radius. Place the compass point on Q and draw an arc that intersects the initial arc. The point of intersection forms the 60-degree angle with P and Q.

questions.questionText: When constructing an angle bisector of angle XYZ, you first draw an arc from Y that intersects YX at A and YZ at B. What is the next step?
options: Draw a line from Y to the midpoint of AB.
From A, draw an arc into the angle's interior, then from B, draw another arc with the same radius intersecting the first.
Measure the angle XYZ with a protractor and divide by two.
Extend the lines YX and YZ.
questions.explanation: To bisect an angle, draw arcs from the points where the first arc intersects the angle's arms (A and B), ensuring both arcs have the same radius and intersect within the angle's interior. A line from the vertex Y through this intersection point bisects the angle.

questions.questionText: Which of the following angles cannot be constructed accurately using only a ruler and compasses?
options: 45 degrees
75 degrees
105 degrees
50 degrees
questions.explanation: Angles that can be constructed with ruler and compass are those whose measure is of the form k * (360 / 2^n) where k and n are integers, and also sums/differences of such angles. 50 degrees is not constructible by these methods. 45° (90° bisected), 75° (60° + 15°), and 105° (60° + 45° or 90° + 15°) are constructible.

questions.questionText: You are constructing a triangle ABC where AB = 7 cm, AC = 5 cm, and BC = 6 cm. After drawing the line segment AB, what is the next step?
options: Draw an arc of radius 5 cm from point B.
Draw an arc of radius 6 cm from point A.
Draw an arc of radius 5 cm from point A and an arc of radius 6 cm from point B.
Measure 5 cm along the line AB and mark C.
questions.explanation: To construct a triangle given three side lengths, draw the first side. Then, from one endpoint, draw an arc with the length of the second side, and from the other endpoint, draw an arc with the length of the third side. The intersection of these arcs gives the third vertex.

questions.questionText: To construct a line perpendicular to a given line L passing through a point P not on L, what is the initial step?
options: Place the compass point on P and draw an arc intersecting L at two points.
Draw a line from P to any point on L.
Measure the shortest distance from P to L.
Place the compass point on a point on L and draw an arc through P.
questions.explanation: To construct a perpendicular from a point to a line, place the compass point on P, open it wide enough to intersect the line L at two distinct points. These two points will then be used to construct the perpendicular bisector of the segment between them, which will pass through P.

questions.questionText: What type of locus is formed by all points equidistant from two fixed points A and B?
options: A circle with center A.
The perpendicular bisector of the line segment AB.
A line parallel to AB.
A circle with diameter AB.
questions.explanation: The locus of points equidistant from two fixed points A and B is the perpendicular bisector of the line segment connecting A and B.

questions.questionText: When constructing a parallel line to a given line through a point P not on the line, one common method involves constructing a corresponding angle. After drawing a transversal through P intersecting the given line, what is the key next step?
options: Measure the angle formed by the transversal and the given line, then replicate it at P.
Draw a perpendicular from P to the given line.
Draw a circle centered at P.
Extend the given line.
questions.explanation: A common method for constructing parallel lines is to use corresponding angles. Measure the angle formed by a transversal intersecting the given line, then construct an identical angle at point P on the transversal.

questions.questionText: To construct a square with a given side length, say 5 cm, after drawing the first side AB of 5 cm, what is the next essential construction at points A and B?
options: Construct 45-degree angles at A and B.
Construct perpendicular lines at A and B.
Measure 5 cm along a diagonal from A.
Draw arcs of radius 5 cm from A and B to find the center.
questions.explanation: A square has four right angles. Therefore, after drawing the first side, you must construct perpendicular lines (90-degree angles) at both endpoints of that side to establish the adjacent sides.

questions.questionText: You are asked to construct the locus of points less than 3 cm from a point X. What shape will this locus represent?
options: A line segment.
The area inside a circle with radius 3 cm centered at X.
A circle with radius 3 cm centered at X.
A perpendicular bisector.
questions.explanation: The locus of points 'less than' a certain distance from a fixed point describes the entire interior region of a circle. If it were 'exactly' 3 cm, it would be the circumference of the circle.

questions.questionText: When constructing an angle of 90 degrees using only a ruler and compasses, what fundamental construction is being performed?
options: Angle bisection.
Line bisection.
Perpendicular line from a point on a line.
Parallel line construction.
questions.explanation: Constructing a 90-degree angle at a point on a line is equivalent to constructing a perpendicular line that passes through that point on the given line.

questions.questionText: To construct a regular hexagon inside a circle, what is the relationship between the radius of the circle and the side length of the hexagon?
options: The side length is half the radius.
The side length is equal to the radius.
The side length is twice the radius.
The side length is root 3 times the radius.
questions.explanation: In a regular hexagon inscribed in a circle, each side length is equal to the radius of the circle. This allows for simple construction by marking off the radius around the circumference.
